origin.name: Israel
origin.investigating_authority: Israel Police and Israel Institute of Forensic Medicine
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Unexpected, unnatural, or suspicious deaths. Post-mortem contested by Orthodox Jewish communities.
origin.religious_sensitivity_note: Jewish law requires burial as soon as possible. Post-mortem is deeply contested in Orthodox communities. Families should be aware this may cause tension with authorities.
origin.embalming_note: Embalming is not standard in Israel due to Jewish burial traditions. Must be specifically arranged for international repatriation.
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is not available in Israel. Jewish law prohibits cremation.
origin.key_document: Teudat Petira (Israeli death certificate) with certified English translation
origin.source: FCDO Israel travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Tel Aviv guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Teudat Petira (Israeli death certificate) with certified English translation
special_rules: Death certificate (teudat petira) is in Hebrew. Certified English translation mandatory.
Cremation is not available in Israel. All repatriations are full body.
Embalming must be specifically requested as it is not standard Jewish burial practice.
Jewish families should be aware that post-mortem is contested in Orthodox communities, which may affect speed of documentation.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Israeli documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Tel Aviv can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Israel to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Israel. Repatriation to the UK takes 7-14 days. Hebrew documentation and Tel Aviv cargo flights explained. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
key_documents: Teudat Petira with certified English translation
special_rules: El Al operates a direct TLV to DUB route.
Irish Embassy in Tel Aviv handles consular matters.
Certified English translation required identically to UK repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Israeli documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Tel Aviv can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Israel to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Israel and needs to come home to Ireland. 7-14 days. Hebrew documentation and Tel Aviv cargo flights handled. Contact us 24/7.
